title: Spin-coherence characterization of boron vacancy defects in hexagonal boron nitride with broadband microwave pulses
authors: Yuki Nakamura, Takuya Iwasaki, Shu Nakaharai, Shinichi Ogawa, Yukinori Morita, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Kento Sasaki, Kensuke Kobayashi
abstract: Negatively charged boron vacancy (VB-) defects in hexagonal boron nitride (hBN) are promising for nanoscale-proximity quantum sensing. To evaluate their performance, it is important to characterize the spin coherence times T2* and T2. In this study, we realized sub-GHz Rabi oscillations of VB- using an isotopically enriched hBN thin film directly stamped onto a narrow gold wire. Using these strong microwave pulses, we performed Ramsey interference and Hahn echo measurements. The Ramsey interference signal showed Gaussian-like decay, yielding T2* = 13.8 ns. The Hahn echo measurement gave T2 = 108.7 ns and a stretch factor of α= 1.25. These results experimentally clarify the spin coherence properties of VB- and provide an effective method for evaluating the coherence of spin defects in van der Waals thin films with broad resonance linewidths.
summary: This study characterizes the spin coherence of boron vacancy defects in hBN using broadband microwave pulses. By measuring T2* and T2 times, the researchers established an effective method for evaluating spin defects in van der Waals thin films. These findings are crucial for developing hBN-based nanoscale quantum sensors.
main_problem: Characterizing the spin coherence times (T2* and T2) of negatively charged boron vacancy (VB-) defects in hBN for use in nanoscale quantum sensing.
main_result: The study achieved sub-GHz Rabi oscillations and measured spin coherence times of T2* = 13.8 ns and T2 = 108.7 ns using broadband microwave pulses.
method: The researchers used broadband microwave pulses on an isotopically enriched hBN thin film stamped onto a gold wire to perform Ramsey interference and Hahn echo measurements.
model_or_system: The physical system consists of negatively charged boron vacancy (VB-) defects in an isotopically enriched hexagonal boron nitride (hBN) thin film integrated with a narrow gold wire.
key_observables: Rabi oscillations, Ramsey interference signal (Gaussian-like decay), Hahn echo signal, and the stretch factor (alpha).
important_parameters: T2* = 13.8 ns, T2 = 108.7 ns, stretch factor alpha = 1.25, and sub-GHz Rabi frequency.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the potential of VB- in hBN for sensing, describes the experimental setup using gold wires and isotopically enriched films, presents the results of Rabi, Ramsey, and Hahn echo measurements, and concludes with the implications for evaluating spin defects in van der Waals materials.
why_interesting: This paper is relevant for researchers in open quantum systems and quantum optics as it provides experimental characterization of decoherence mechanisms and spin-coherence dynamics in a solid-state platform suitable for quantum sensing.
